instruments.evaluation_form_ref_assumption_note: Assumed from application form filename naming convention (af_→ef_); evaluation_forms/ directory not in declared inputs for this skill invocation. Must be verified against actual evaluation_forms/ directory contents.
instruments.max_work_packages_note: No maximum work package count stated in form. Form states: 'the number of work packages should be proportionate to the scale and complexity of the project.' Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B p.13 of 23.
instruments.project_duration_months_note: Not specified in application form template; varies by call. Duration field in Part A Section 1 is entered by applicant. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part A p.4 of 24.
instruments.max_deliverable_count_note: No maximum deliverable count stated in form. Table 3.1c instruction states: 'Only include deliverables that you consider essential for effective project monitoring.' Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B p.17 of 23.
instruments.part_b_page_limit_hard_note: Directly stated in form: 'The title, list of participants and sections 1, 2 and 3, together, should not be longer than 40 pages. For topics using lump sum funding, the limit is 45 pages.' Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B p.3 of 23. Enforced automatically; excess pages hidden after deadline.

field_requirements: Acronym — form explicitly labels field 'Acronym is mandatory'
Proposal title — max 200 characters with spaces; must be understandable for non-specialists; characters < > " & not accepted and will be removed
Duration in months — estimated duration of project in full months
Fixed keyword 1 — pre-set by IT system based on call; read-only
Fixed keyword 2 — pre-set by IT system based on call; read-only
Free keywords — max 200 characters with spaces; additional scope detail

Abstract — short description of proposal objectives, how they will be achieved, and relevance to Work Programme; must be short and precise; no confidential information; plain typed text only; if proposal in other language an English version must be included in Part B

Prior submission declaration — has this proposal or a very similar one been submitted in the past 2 years under any EU programme including current call? (Yes/No); if Yes: proposal reference or contract number
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — Acronym field explicitly marked 'Acronym is mandatory' in form; Section 1 is listed in Part A Table of Contents as a required section. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part A pp.3-4 of 24.

field_requirements: Declaration 1: explicit consent of all applicants on participation and content of this proposal (checkbox)
Declaration 2: information correct and complete; project activities not started before proposal submission unless explicitly authorised in call conditions (checkbox)

Declaration 3: fully compliant with eligibility criteria; not subject to exclusion grounds under EU Financial Regulation; financial and operational capacity to carry out the proposed project (checkbox)
Declaration 4: all communication will be made through the Funding and Tenders Portal electronic exchange system (checkbox)
Declaration 5: Funding and Tenders Portal Terms and Conditions and Privacy Statement read, understood, and accepted for processing of personal data (checkbox)

Declaration 6: proposal complies with ethical principles including ALLEA European Code of Conduct for Research Integrity; Charter of Fundamental Rights; European Convention on Human Rights and Supplementary Protocols; appropriate procedures in place to foster responsible research practices and handle misconduct (checkbox)
Declaration 7: exclusive focus on civil applications except where military applications explicitly permitted in call conditions; compliance with Regulation 2021/821 for dual-use items (checkbox)

Declaration 8: activities do not aim at human cloning for reproductive purposes; do not intend to modify heritable genetic heritage of human beings (exception: cancer treatment of gonads); do not intend to create human embryos solely for research or stem cell procurement including by somatic cell nuclear transfer; do not lead to destruction of human embryos (checkbox)
Declaration 9: activities carried out outside the Union would also have been allowed in at least one EU Member State (checkbox)

Declaration 10 — LUMP SUM GRANTS ONLY: understanding and acceptance that EU lump sum grants must be reliable proxies for actual costs; detailed budget established in accordance with usual cost accounting practices and AGA art 6; ineligible costs excluded; purchases and subcontracting done taking into account best value for money and free of conflict of interest (checkbox)
sections.note: mandatory: true — form explicitly states 'These declarations can be filled in by any coordinator contact(s). All declarations are mandatory.' Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part A pp.5-6 of 24.

field_requirements: List of participating organisations — legal name, country for each participant

Organisation data per participant: PIC, legal name, short name, address (street, town, postcode, country, webpage), specific legal statuses (public, non-profit, international organisation, international organisation of European interest, secondary/higher education, research organisation), SME self-declared status, SME self-assessment, SME validation
Departments carrying out the proposed work — department name and address; 'not applicable' checkbox option
Links with other participants — type of link (same group / controls / is controlled by) and participant name
Main contact person — title, gender (Woman/Man/Non binary), first name, last name, e-mail, position in organisation, department, address, phone 1, phone 2
Other contact persons — first name, last name, e-mail, phone (table)

Researchers involved in the proposal — title, first name, last name, gender, nationality, e-mail, career stage (Category A/B/C/D per Frascati Manual 2015), role in project (Leading/Team member), reference identifier, type of identifier (ORCID/ResearcherID/Other)

Role of participating organisation — checkboxes (multiple selection): project management; communication, dissemination and engagement; provision of research and technology infrastructure; co-definition of research and market needs; civil society representative; policy maker or regulator incl. standardisation body; research performer; technology developer; testing/validation of approaches and ideas; prototyping and demonstration; IPR management incl. technology transfer; public procurer of results; private buyer of results; finance provider (public or private); education and training; contributions from social sciences and/or humanities; Other (specify, 50 character limit)

List of up to 5 publications, widely-used datasets, software, goods, services, or other achievements relevant to call content — type (Publication/Dataset/Software/Good/Service/Other achievement), short description including DOI or PID where available
List of up to 5 most relevant previous projects or activities connected to subject of proposal — name of project or activity, short description
Description of significant infrastructure and/or major items of technical equipment relevant to proposed work — name, short description

Gender equality plan (GEP) — Does the organisation have a GEP covering minimum process requirements (publication, dedicated resources, data collection and monitoring, training) and content areas (work-life balance, gender balance in leadership, gender equality in recruitment and career progression, integration of gender dimension into research/teaching content, measures against gender-based violence including sexual harassment)? (Yes/No) — eligibility criterion for public bodies, higher education establishments, and research organisations from Member States and Associated Countries
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — Section 2 listed in Part A Table of Contents as required section; form states 'The forms must be filled in for each proposal using the templates available in the Submission System.' Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part A pp.7-14 of 24.

field_requirements: Budget table per participant — participant number, participant name, country
A. Personnel costs (€)
B. Subcontracting costs (€)
C.1 Travel and subsistence (€)
C.2 Equipment (€)
C.3 Other goods, works and services (€)
D.x Specific cost category (€) — call-specific other cost categories
E. Indirect costs (€) — 25% flat rate on eligible costs excluding subcontracting (b) and financial support to third parties (d1)
Total eligible costs (€)
Funding rate (U)
Maximum EU contribution to eligible costs (l = U × h)
Requested EU contribution to eligible costs — Requested grant amount (m)(n)
Income generated by the action (o)
Financial contributions (q)
Own resources (r)
Total estimated income (s = (n)+(o)+(q)+(r))

D. Other cost categories breakdown table per participant: D.1 financial support to third parties — actual costs; D.2 internally invoiced goods and services — unit costs (usual accounting practices); D.3 transnational access to research infrastructures — unit costs; D.4 virtual access to research infrastructures — unit costs; D.5 PCP/PPI procurement costs — actual costs; D.6 Euratom Cofund staff mobility costs — unit costs; D.7 ERC additional funding — actual costs; D.8 ERC additional funding (subcontracting, FSTP, internally invoiced goods and services) — actual costs
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — Section 3 (Budget) listed in Part A Table of Contents as required section; budget table is a required submission element. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part A pp.15-16 of 24.

field_requirements: Ethics issues table — form states: 'This table should be completed as an essential part of your proposal.' 9 categories with Yes/No responses; page reference to be provided for each triggered issue

Category 1 — Human embryonic stem cells and human embryos: hESCs involved; directly derived from embryos; previously established cell lines; registered in European registry; use of human embryos; will activity lead to their destruction

Category 2 — Humans: human participants; volunteers for nonmedical studies; healthy volunteers for medical studies; patients for medical studies; potentially vulnerable individuals or groups; children/minors; other persons unable to give informed consent; interventions on study participants (physical/imaging/behavioural); invasive techniques; collection of biological samples; clinical study per EU 536/2014; clinical trial; low-intervention clinical trial

Category 3 — Human cells/tissues (not covered by section 1): use of human cells or tissues; embryonic/foetal; commercially available; obtained within project; obtained from another institution; obtained from biobank

Category 4 — Personal data: processing of personal data; special categories (sexual lifestyle, ethnicity, genetic/biometric/health data, political/religious/philosophical beliefs); profiling/systematic monitoring/large scale processing; further processing of previously collected data; planned export from EU; planned import from non-EU countries; processing related to criminal convictions or offences
Category 5 — Animals: vertebrates; non-human primates; genetically modified; cloned farm animals; endangered species

Category 6 — Non-EU countries: activities in non-EU countries; potential ethics issues; use of local resources; import/export of material; low and/or lower-middle income countries involvement; risk to individuals in country
Category 7 — Environment, health and safety: substances/processes causing harm to environment/animals/plants; endangered fauna/flora/protected areas; substances/processes causing harm to humans

Category 8 — Artificial intelligence: development/deployment/use of AI-based systems; if yes detail whether this raises ethical concerns related to human rights and values and how this will be addressed
Category 9 — Other ethics issues: any other ethics issues to be taken into consideration (max 1000 characters)
Ethics confirmation checkbox: 'I confirm that I have taken into account all ethics issues above and that, if any ethics issues apply, I will complete the ethics self-assessment'
Ethics self-assessment (if triggered): ethical dimension of objectives/methodology/likely impact; compliance with ethical principles and relevant legislation

Security issues table: Category 1 EU classified information (EUCI); Category 2 misuse potential (crime/terrorism/CBRN); Category 3 other security issues including national security restrictions (max 1000 characters)
Security self-assessment (if triggered): measures to solve/avoid security issues (max 5000 characters)
sections.note: mandatory: true — form explicitly states 'This table should be completed as an essential part of your proposal.' Section 4 listed in Part A Table of Contents as required section. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part A pp.17-23 of 24.

field_requirements: Two-stage calls — are there substantial differences compared to the stage-1 proposal? (Yes/No); if Yes: list substantial differences and reasons for Partnership, Budget, Approach (checkboxes with free text)

Clinical trials — are clinical studies/trials/investigations included in the work plan of this project? (Yes/No); if Yes: upload dedicated annex 'Essential information for clinical studies/trials/investigations' (Word template from Funding and Tenders Portal download templates)
Clinical study identifiers — please give a short title, an acronym or a unique identifier to each clinical study/trial/investigation included in the work plan
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — Section 5 listed in Part A Table of Contents as required section; required for all proposals to address two-stage and clinical trials conditions. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part A p.24 of 24.

sections.section_name: Title of the Proposal and List of Participants
field_requirements: Title of the Proposal — formatted header block

List of participants table: Participant No. (1=Coordinator, 2, 3, ...), Participant organisation name, Country — form states: 'Please use the same participant numbering and name as that used in the administrative proposal forms'
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — form states 'The consortium members are listed in part A of the proposal (application forms). A summary list should also be provided in the table below.' Indicative page count '[e.g. 1 page]' is explicitly indicative only; page_limit set to null. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B p.6 of 23.

field_requirements: Parent section for Excellence evaluation criterion

Aspects to be taken into account: (1) Clarity and pertinence of the project's objectives, and the extent to which the proposed work is ambitious, and goes beyond the state of the art; (2) Soundness of the proposed methodology, including the underlying concepts, models, assumptions, interdisciplinary approaches, appropriate consideration of the gender dimension in research and innovation content, and the quality of open science practices, including sharing and management of research outputs and engagement of citizens, civil society and end users where appropriate
Note: aspects taken into account only to the extent that the proposed work is within the scope of the work programme topic
Contains subsections B.1.1 Objectives and ambition and B.1.2 Methodology
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — form states 'The structure of this template must be followed when preparing your proposal.' Section 1 corresponds to the Excellence evaluation criterion. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B p.7 of 23.

field_requirements: Brief description of project objectives: pertinence to work programme topic; measurability and verifiability; realistic achievability

How the project goes beyond state-of-the-art: exceptional ground-breaking R&I; novel concepts and approaches; new products, services, business and organisational models; advance illustrated by reference to available products/services on market where relevant; reference to patent or publication search carried out
R&I maturity positioning on spectrum from 'idea to application' or 'lab to market'
Technology Readiness Level (TRL) indication at project start and end where applicable; expectations differ for RIAs at lower TRL versus IAs at higher TRLs
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — inferred from 'The structure of this template must be followed.' Per-section page indication '[e.g. 4 pages]' is explicitly indicative only; form states 'The number of pages included in each section of this template is only indicative'; page_limit set to null. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B p.7 of 23.

field_requirements: Overall methodology description: concepts, models and assumptions underpinning the work; how methodology enables delivery of objectives; important challenges and how to overcome them; presented as narrative (detailed tasks/WPs described under Implementation)
National or international R&I activities whose results feed into the project and how that link will be established
Interdisciplinary approach: how expertise and methods from different disciplines are brought together; justification if inter-disciplinary approach considered unnecessary
Social sciences and humanities integration: show role of SSH disciplines or justification for non-relevance — for topics where work programme indicates need

Gender dimension in R&I content: how sex/gender analysis (biological characteristics and social/cultural factors) is taken into account in research and innovation content; justification if not considered relevant — mandatory except for topics identified in work programme as not requiring gender dimension integration
Open science practices: choice of practices; how practices adapted to nature of work and will increase chances of project delivering on objectives; justification if none appropriate
Data management plan (DMP): to be provided as distinct deliverable within first 6 months; must ensure research data is findable, accessible, interoperable and reusable (FAIR)
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — inferred from 'The structure of this template must be followed.' Per-section page indication '[e.g. 14 pages]' is explicitly indicative only; page_limit set to null. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B pp.7-8 of 23.

field_requirements: Parent section for Impact evaluation criterion

Aspects to be taken into account: (1) Credibility of the pathways to achieve the expected outcomes and impacts specified in the work programme; (2) Suitability and quality of the measures to maximise expected outcomes and impacts, as set out in the dissemination and exploitation plan, including communication activities
Results should make contribution to expected outcomes (medium term) and wider expected impacts in respective destination (longer term)
Contains subsections B.2.1, B.2.2, and optional B.2.3
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — inferred from 'The structure of this template must be followed.' Section 2 corresponds to the Impact evaluation criterion. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B p.9 of 23.

field_requirements: Narrative explaining how project results are expected to make a difference in terms of impact beyond immediate scope and duration of project

(a) Unique contribution to: (1) outcomes specified in the work programme topic and (2) wider impacts specified in respective destinations — be specific, referring to effects of this project not R&I in general
Target groups: specific interest groups or segments of society that would benefit — break down general target groups into particular interest groups or segments relevant to this project

(b) Requirements and potential barriers arising from factors beyond scope and duration of project: other R&I work within and beyond Horizon Europe; regulatory environment; targeted markets; user behaviour — indicate if factors may evolve over time; describe mitigating measures within or beyond project
Quantified estimates where possible and meaningful
Note: critical risks inherent to project management described under Implementation section 3.1, not here
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — inferred from 'The structure of this template must be followed.' Per-section page indication '[e.g. 3 pages]' is explicitly indicative only; page_limit set to null. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B p.9 of 23.

sections.section_name: Measures to maximise impact - Dissemination, exploitation and communication
field_requirements: Plan for dissemination and exploitation including communication activities — admissibility condition unless work programme topic explicitly states otherwise; more detailed plan required as mandatory project deliverable within 6 months of grant signature and updated periodically

Dissemination, exploitation and communication measures: target groups (scientific community, end users, financial actors, public at large); measures proportionate to scale of project; actions during and after project end; standardisation activities where relevant; follow-up of project after completion; justification of why each measure is best suited to reach each target group
For Innovation Actions: describe measures for a plausible path to commercialise the innovations
Communication measures: objectives, start date, tools and channels to reach each target group throughout project lifetime; main messages and channels
IP strategy: management of intellectual property; foreseen protection measures (patents, design rights, copyright, trade secrets); how IP would support exploitation
If exploitation primarily in non-associated third countries: justification that exploitation is still in the Union's interest
Policy feedback: contribution to designing/monitoring/reviewing/rectifying existing policy or shaping/supporting new policy initiatives
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — form explicitly states 'Please remember that this plan is an admissibility condition, unless the work programme topic explicitly states otherwise.' Per-section page indication '[e.g. 3 pages, excluding section 2.3]' is explicitly indicative only; page_limit set to null. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B pp.9-10 of 23.

field_requirements: Impact canvas visual summary presenting key elements of the project impact pathway and measures to maximise impact

Upper row: Specific Needs (what specific needs triggered this project?), Expected Results (what do you expect to generate by end of project?), D&E&C Measures (what dissemination/exploitation/communication measures will you apply to results?)

Lower row: Target Groups (who will use or further up-take results? who will benefit?), Outcomes (what change expected after successful dissemination and exploitation of project results to target groups?), Impacts (what are the expected wider scientific/economic/societal effects contributing to expected impacts in respective destination in work programme?)
sections.note: mandatory: false — form explicitly states '2.3 Summary (OPTIONAL – Please remove this section if not used).' Direct form marking present in instruction pages (Part B p.11 of 23) and in the actual fillable template (Part B Page 2 of [Page limit]). Version 5.1 change log confirms: 'Added mention of remove if not used'. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B pp.11-12 of 23 and template pp.2-3 of [Page limit].

sections.section_name: Quality and efficiency of the implementation
field_requirements: Parent section for Quality and efficiency of the implementation evaluation criterion

Aspects to be taken into account: (1) Quality and effectiveness of the work plan, assessment of risks, appropriateness of the effort assigned to work packages, and the resources overall; (2) Capacity and role of each participant, and extent to which the consortium as a whole brings together the necessary expertise
Contains subsections B.3.1 Work plan and resources and B.3.2 Capacity of participants and consortium as a whole
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — inferred from 'The structure of this template must be followed.' Section 3 corresponds to the Quality and efficiency of the implementation evaluation criterion. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B p.13 of 23.

field_requirements: Brief presentation of overall work plan structure
Timing of work packages and components — Gantt chart or similar
Graphical presentation of inter-WP dependencies — PERT chart or similar
Table 3.1a — List of work packages: Work Package No, Work Package Title, Lead Participant No, Lead Participant Short Name, Person-Months, Start Month, End Month

Table 3.1b — Work package description (for each WP): Work package number, Work package title, Objectives, Description of work (broken down into tasks where appropriate; lead partner and role of participants; for each task quantify amount of work; justify resources; clarify why work needed and who will do it; deliverables linked to each WP listed in Table 3.1c — no need to repeat in 3.1b)

Table 3.1c — List of deliverables: Number (convention: WP-number.deliverable-number e.g. 4.2), Deliverable name, Short description, Work package number, Short name of lead participant, Type (R=document/report; DEM=demonstrator/pilot/prototype/plan designs; DEC=websites/patents/press/media/videos; DATA=data sets/microdata; DMP=data management plan; ETHICS=ethics deliverables; SECURITY=security deliverables; OTHER=software/technical diagram/algorithms/models), Dissemination level (PU=public fully open; SEN=sensitive limited under Grant Agreement; Classified R-UE/EU-R; Classified C-UE/EU-C; Classified S-UE/EU-S), Delivery date in months from project start — DMP and dissemination/exploitation plan required as distinct deliverables within first 6 months

Table 3.1d — List of milestones: Milestone number, Milestone name, Related work packages, Due date in month from project start, Means of verification (show how milestone attainment will be confirmed; refer to indicators if appropriate)

Table 3.1e — Critical risks for implementation: Description of risk with level of likelihood (Low/Medium/High) and level of severity (Low/Medium/High), Work packages involved, Proposed risk-mitigation measures — critical risk defined as plausible event or issue that could have high adverse impact on ability to achieve project objectives

Table 3.1f — Summary of staff effort: Person-months per participant per work package and total person-months per participant; identify work package leader per WP by showing relevant person-month figure in bold
Table 3.1g — Subcontracting costs items: per participant — cost (€), description of tasks and justification; note: core tasks of project should not be sub-contracted

Table 3.1h — Purchase costs items (major equipment costs): per participant — cost (€), justification — required when equipment costs exceed 15% of personnel costs for that participant per budget table in proposal Part A
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — inferred from 'The structure of this template must be followed.' Per-section page indication '[e.g. 12 pages (17 pages for topics using lump sum funding) — including tables]' is explicitly indicative only; page_limit set to null. All tables, figures, references and other elements pertaining to these sections counted against overall 40-page Part B limit. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B pp.13-19 of 23.

sections.section_name: Capacity of participants and consortium as a whole
field_requirements: Consortium description: match to project objectives; disciplinary and inter-disciplinary knowledge; expertise in SSH, open science practices, gender aspects of R&I; inclusion of affiliated entities and associated partners if any
Access to critical infrastructure needed to carry out project activities
Complementarity of partners: how members complement one another and cover the value chain where appropriate
Individual contributions: valid role and adequate resources of each partner to fulfil that role
Industrial/commercial involvement: consistency with exploitation measures from section 2.2 and how it helps achieve those measures

Other countries and international organisations: if any participant requesting EU funding is not automatically eligible (not from EU Member State, Associated Country, or exhaustive list in Work Programme General Annexes B), explain why their participation is essential to successfully carry out the project
Note: individual participants described in Part A Section 2 — do not repeat that information here
sections.assumption_note: mandatory: true — inferred from 'The structure of this template must be followed.' Per-section page indication '[e.g. 3 pages]' is explicitly indicative only; page_limit set to null. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B p.14 of 23.

field_requirements: Clinical trials annex — 'Essential information for clinical studies/trials/investigations': Word template published on Funding and Tenders Portal under download templates; includes relevant information of each clinical study/trial/investigation in work plan
Financial support to third parties annex
Security-sensitive calls annex — information on security aspects for calls flagged as security sensitive
Ethics extended self-assessment annex — for cases where character limit in Part A Section 4 is insufficient for serious ethics issues requiring more information
sections.note: mandatory: false — form states 'Some calls may ask to upload annexes to proposal part B. The annexes must be uploaded as separate documents in the submission system.' Annex requirements are call-specific, not universally mandatory. Source: af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf Part B p.20 of 23.
